editorsNote: Atlético sharpened the question without settling it. Barca still have Espanyol to restore their authority and then Madrid to answer properly, so the useful work this week is simple: recover the spacing, start with force, and make the tie feel football-sized again before panic gets to narrate it.

items.headline: Montjuic Needs a Louder Start
items.summary: Montjuic changes the timing of belief, so Barca still need to ignite matches earlier than they once did.

items.headline: Barca's Rest Defense Is Learning to Breathe
items.summary: Calmer spacing behind the ball is turning risky transitions into survivable moments again.

items.headline: Five lines after Atlético, before Espanyol
items.summary: The Brief compresses the loss, the league reset, and the second-leg pressure into one clean read.

items.headline: Espanyol is the first reset point
items.summary: Apr 11 is the immediate chance to restore tempo before the return leg pulls the mood in every direction again.

items.headline: Madrid is where the week turns
items.summary: Apr 14 decides whether the first-leg loss becomes panic or just the hard edge of a still-live tie.

notes: Planning issue 2 record only; keep internal until the active dispatch is explicitly promoted.
